title: Chicken Cacciatore (Hunter-style Chicken)

ingredients.title: For the Chicken:
items: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.title: For the Sauce:
items: 1 (28 oz) can whole peeled tomatoes, crushed by hand
1/2 tsp crushed red pepper flakes (optional, for a gentle heat)

ingredients.title: For Serving:
items: Cooked polenta, risotto, or pappardelle pasta

instruction.title: Prepare the Chicken:
steps: Pat chicken pieces dry with paper towels.
Season generously with salt and black pepper.
In a large, heavy-bottomed skillet or Dutch oven, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Sear chicken until golden brown on all sides, about 5–7 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.

instruction.title: Sauté the Vegetables:
steps: In the same pan, add onions, carrots, and celery. Sauté until softened, 5–6 minutes.
Add garlic and bell peppers, cooking 1–2 more minutes until fragrant.

instruction.title: Deglaze & Build the Sauce:
steps: Pour in white wine, scraping up browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Let wine reduce by half, about 3–4 minutes.
Stir in crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, oregano, thyme, bay leaves, and red pepper flakes.

instruction.title: Simmer the Chicken:
steps: Return chicken to the pan, nestling pieces into the sauce.
Pour in chicken stock if needed to cover 2/3 of the chicken.
Bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook for 35–45 minutes until chicken is tender and cooked through.

instruction.title: Finish the Sauce:
steps: Stir in capers, olives, and fresh parsley.
Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.

instruction.title: Serve:
steps: Plate chicken over pasta, polenta, or risotto.
Garnish with fresh basil leaves and a drizzle of high-quality olive oil.

chefTips: Brown the chicken well: Maillard reaction is key for flavor. Don’t overcrowd the pan.
Use bone-in, skin-on pieces: They add depth and richness to the sauce.
Simmer slowly: Low heat ensures the chicken stays tender and absorbs the sauce flavors.
Balance acidity: If sauce tastes too tangy, add a small pinch of sugar to mellow it.
Rest before serving: Let chicken sit in the sauce off the heat for 5 minutes for maximum flavor.
